-Neutron-carbon interaction, total and elastic
scattering differential cross sections and phase shift
analysis, 2.1 to 4.7 MeV.-
Van de graaff accelerator at legnaro, padua.
Proton-tritium, gas target.
Average neutron energy spread is about 40 KeV,
uncertainty of absolute neutron energy scale is 10 KeV
.Energies of neutrons were calculated from proton
energies, taking into account the energy lost in the
target.  The energy values were checked by two C-12
resonances at 2.077 and 2.95 MeV.
Time-of-flight, path= 3 m. Transmission exp.

Date and reference are corrected.
Last checking has been done.

LIQUID SCINTILLATOR NE 213 IN A CAPSULE
10 CM WIDE 5 CM THICK, SEEN BY A XP 1040 PHOTOMULTI-
PLIER.
.NEUTRON FLUX WAS MONITORED WITH A SCINTILLATION
COUNTER, CONTROLLED BY A CURRENT INTEGRATOR OF PROTON
BEAM.

AFTER INTEGRATION OVER SOLID ANGLE OF EXPERIMENTAL
ANGULAR DISTRIBUTION.  THRESHOLD OF INELASTIC SCATTER-
ING IS 4.8 MEV.
EFFICIENCY CURVE WAS DETERMINED BY N-P
SCATTERING ON A CYLINDRICAL POLYETHYLENE SCATTERER.

THE NORMALIZATION OF THE TWO COUNTS - SAMPLE IN AND

BEAM CURRENT.

RESULT BY KFK GROUP, CIERJACKS ET AL., NEUTRON CROSS
SECTIONS AND TECHNOLOGY, NBS SPEC.PUB.299(1968)P.743.
